question_text: Two identical bar magnets are placed horizontally on a frictionless table, end to end, 10 cm apart. The north pole of magnet A faces the south pole of magnet B. If the force between them is 0.5 N, which of the following statements is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: Magnet A experiences a 0.5 N force, but magnet B experiences a different force

options.label: B
options.text: Both magnets experience 0.5 N attractive forces on each other

options.label: C
options.text: Magnet B experiences a 0.5 N force, but magnet A experiences no force

options.label: D
options.text: The forces are equal only if the magnets are the same size

explanation: By Newton's third law, the forces that two magnets exert on each other are equal in magnitude and opposite in direction. Both magnets experience the same 0.5 N force (attractive, since unlike poles attract).
